evals.prompt: A product team is about to write the hero copy for their B2B SaaS landing page. Their current copy is generic ('Build better software, faster'). They describe the vibe as 'direct but warmer than most developer tools — we want to feel like a trusted colleague, not a vendor pitching.' Name the copy direction for this surface.
evals.expected_output: A small set of NAMED copy goals, each a noun phrase a non-designer can recall, RANKED so a tie can break, with a single dominant goal that wins when goals conflict. Each goal grounded in at least one stable referent (persona language, a named copy precedent, or a persuasion standard). At least one arbitration rule recorded (which goal wins which conflict and why). No finished copy strings, formula tables, or pre-written headlines. VoC posture declared (directional if elicited inline). Output path docs/design/copy-direction/<slug>.md stated or implied.
assertions: Names the primary reader type on this landing page surface and writes or references a copy JTBD sentence
Produces at least two named copy goals, each a short noun phrase (not a sentence or a single vague word)
Ranks the goals and names a single dominant goal that breaks ties
Each goal has at least one stable referent: persona language, a named copy precedent, or a named persuasion standard
Records at least one arbitration rule (conflict type → winner → reason)
Does NOT produce finished copy strings, formula tables, or pre-written headlines
If VoC is absent, flags goals as 'directional — not backed by VoC research'
Artifact path docs/design/copy-direction/<slug>.md stated or implied
Does NOT produce SEO keyword targeting, brand identity documentation, or UI string copy
